data.narrative: Completed Module 4 by shipping structured JSON logging, a Prometheus-style /metrics endpoint, and low-cardinality request metrics. I also hardened observability by documenting alert rules and by validating both logs and metrics with tests.

dimension_scores.observation: Identified the need for production-grade observability rather than only basic HTTP request handling.
dimension_scores.evidence: Added structured logging, request_id propagation, /metrics, and alert documentation to the service.

dimension_scores.observation: Considered operational dependencies and their visibility by exposing metrics for both request performance and service-level business counts.
dimension_scores.evidence: Implemented url_shortener_links_current_count gauge and request duration histograms to make service health observable.

dimension_scores.observation: Caught a real production risk around high-cardinality metric labels and corrected it with route-template labeling.
dimension_scores.evidence: Rewrote metric label generation to use route templates instead of raw request paths and added a regression test.

dimension_scores.observation: Delivered clear, testable observability improvements without adding unnecessary runtime complexity.
dimension_scores.evidence: The /metrics endpoint and JSON logs are directly verifiable through tests and runtime checks.

dimension_scores.observation: Adapted the instrumentation design when the initial metrics labeling approach risked exploding time series cardinality.
dimension_scores.evidence: Changed from raw path labels to route template labels before committing the service instrumentation.

dimension_scores.observation: Created operational documentation that defines alerts, thresholds, and runbook links for on-call responders.
dimension_scores.evidence: Added docs/alerts.md covering error rate, latency, and business metric drop alerts.

strengths: Built end-to-end observability with structured logs, request metrics, business metrics, and operational alert definitions.
growth_areas: Next, extend the design to graceful degradation and dependency failure handling so observability can drive recovery actions.
data.recommendation: Proceed to Module 5 with a focus on failure mode analysis for database and external dependency outages, then design fallback paths and alerting around degraded behavior.

public_evidence.student_action: Implemented structured JSON logging and request_id propagation.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Added a custom JSON formatter that includes timestamp, service_name, request_id, level, message, and traceback when present.
public_evidence.proof: tests/test_error_handling.py includes checks for JSON logging fields in error logs.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows capability to make logs reliably searchable and correlated to requests in production.

public_evidence.student_action: Added a Prometheus /metrics endpoint and low-cardinality request metrics.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Instrumented http_requests_total, http_request_duration_seconds, and url_shortener_links_current_count with route template labels.
public_evidence.proof: Runtime /metrics output is valid Prometheus text and tests verify histogram buckets and gauge exposure.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows ability to expose production-ready operational metrics without high-cardinality labels.

public_evidence.student_action: Documented alert rules for error rate, latency increase, and business metric drops.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Created actionable alerts with severity, thresholds, and runbook guidance.
public_evidence.proof: docs/alerts.md is present as an operational artifact for on-call responders.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Demonstrates understanding of meaningful alerting and incident response planning.

terminology_gaps.context: Understanding why raw path values are dangerous for metric time series.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Added JSON logs with request_id and traceback fields.
Implemented a Prometheus /metrics endpoint with counters and histograms.

teaching_approaches.detail: Used route templates instead of raw path values to keep time series manageable.
